1.verse_text: Dark-complexioned Lord Krsna, whose form is eternal, blissful, and full of knowledge, formerly danced with the golden-complexioned gopis in the land of Vrndavana. By intently embracing them He attained a golden complexion like theirs. In this golden form He has now appeared in the town of Navadvipa.

clues.clue: This dark-complexioned personality with an eternal form danced with the cowherd damsels in the land of Vraja.
These golden-complexioned damsels of Vraja are the ones with whom the dark-complexioned Lord danced in the past.
This is the land where the dark-complexioned Lord danced with the golden-complexioned cowherd damsels.
This personality attained a golden complexion and appeared in the town of Navadvipa.
This is the town where the golden-complexioned Lord Caitanya has appeared in this age.

2.verse_text: We offer respectful obeisances to Lord Advaita and the other dear associates of the Lord, who are all full of compassion for the fallen souls. We offer our respectful obeisances to Srila Svarupa Damodara Gosvami and the other companions of the Lord. As the Lord Himself is full of auspicious transcendental qualities, so are they. As the Lord Himself is full of mercy, so are they. These great devotees are filled with the sweet nectar of Krsna-consciousness. These great devotees remove the grave sins of this material world.

clues.clue: This dear associate of the Lord removes the grave sins of this material world.
This companion of the Lord is full of auspicious transcendental qualities and sweet nectar.
This personality is known for being full of mercy and having auspicious transcendental qualities.

3.verse_text: We offer our respectful obeisances to Srinatha Cakravarti, our spiritual master, who is very dear to the all-powerful Personality of Godhead. Our spiritual master is like a jewel decorating the earth, or like a splendid moon shining in the brahminical community. Some rare elevated souls in this world are able to understand and relish his nectarean description of Lord Krsna's confidential pastimes in Vrndavana.

clues.clue: He is the teacher of Kavi Karnapura and describes the confidential pastimes of Krsna to the world.
This all-powerful personality participates in the confidential pastimes that take place in the forest of Vrndavana.
This is the specific location where Krsna engages in His confidential pastimes with His devotees.

4.verse_text: With great devotion, I offer my respectful obeisances to my father, Sri Sivanandana, who is like a brilliant lamp shining in the Sena family, and who is counted among the foremost associates of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

clues.clue: He is the father of the author and a prominent associate of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.
The lord who counts this author's father among his foremost and most dedicated associates.
The noble lineage where this author's father shines like a bright and brilliant lamp.

5.verse_text: Srila Svarupa Damodara Gosvami and other great Vaisnava authors have written in their books many descriptions of the great associates of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu, Lord Nityananda, and Lord Advaita. These authors have explained that the associates of Lord Caitanya had previously been cowherd people in Vrndavana during Lord Krsna's advent, and these authors have informed us of the former names of each of these associates of the Lord. I have carefully considered all these writings, and I have also heard the testimonies of the devotees in Bengal, Orissa, and Mathura. Collecting all this evidence, I, Paramananda dasa, write this book describing the previous life of the associates of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

clues.clue: A Vaisnava author who wrote descriptions of the associates of Caitanya, Nityananda, and Advaita.
The place where the cowherd people lived during the time when Krsna appeared there.
The author who compiled this book after considering the writings of various Vaisnava scholars.

6.verse_text: Just as Lord Krsnacandra, who is non-different from the Panca-tattva, formerly appeared in this world, so Lord Caitanya also appeared in this world.

clues.clue: He is the personality who is non-different from the group known as the group of five.
This group of five is described in the text as being non-different from the Supreme Personality.
This personality appeared in the material world in a form that manifested similar to the Supreme Personality.

7.verse_text: The members of the Panca-tattva are non-different from Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu. Had they been different from Him, they would have been known as 'catstayam tattvam (the four truths)'.

clues.clue: This central personality of the group is described as being non-different from the other four members.
This group of five members would be called catstayam tattvam if they were different from Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

8.verse_text: The members of the Pance-tattva are different from Lord Krsna, and then again they are not different from Him. By employing His internal potency, the Lord has created this condition in which the members of the Panca-tattva are simultaneously different from Him and not different from Him.

clues.clue: This group consists of personalities who are simultaneously different from and not different from Lord Krsna.
The supreme personality from whom the group of five entities emanates by His own internal potency.

9.verse_text: Srila Svarupa Damodara Gosvami has described the five members of the Panca-tattva in the following words.

clues.clue: This associate of Caitanya is responsible for describing the five members of the collective absolute truth.
This is the fivefold configuration of the absolute truth as described by Svarupa Damodara Gosvami.

10.verse_text: 'I offer my obeisances unto the Supreme Lord Krsna, who is non-different from His features as a devotee, devotional incarnation, devotional manifestation, pure devotee, and devotional energy.'

clues.clue: This is the Supreme Lord, who is stated to be non-different from His specific devotional features.
In the form of a devotee, this personality is recognized as being non-different from the Supreme Lord.
Representing the devotional incarnation, this figure is non-different from the Supreme Lord who is the source.
This personality is described as the devotional manifestation that is non-different from the Supreme Lord.
He is the pure devotee who is described as being non-different from the Supreme Lord.
He is known as the devotional energy, being non-different from the Supreme Lord.

11.verse_text: I shall now summarily explain the meaning of these words. In this Panca-tattva, the bhakta-rupa (form of a devotee) is Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu, who formerly appeared as Lord Krsna, the son of Nanda Maharaja. The bhakta-svarupa (devotional incarnation) is Lord Nityananda, who formerly appeared in Vrajabhumi as Lord Balarama. The bhaktavatara (devotional manifestation) is Lord Advaita Acarya, who is not different from Lord Sadasiva. The bhaktakhya (pure devotee) is Srinivasa and the other great devotees as well. The bhakta-sakti (devotional energy) is Gadadhara Pandita, the foremost of brahmanas.

clues.clue: This personality appeared in the form of a devotee and was formerly known as Krsna.
This devotional incarnation of the Lord previously lived in Vrajabhumi as the brother of Krsna.
This devotional manifestation is known to be non-different from the identity of Lord Sadasiva.
He is the foremost of all brahmanas and serves as the devotional energy of the Lord.

12.verse_text: Lord Caitanya, Lord Nityananda Avadhuta, and Lord Advaita, are all incarnations of the supremely exalted Personality of Godhead, and They are all known by the title Prabhu (Master). Among Them, Lord Caitanya, who is an ocean of mercy, is known as Mahaprabhu (The Great Master), and the great personalities Lord Nityananda and Lord Advaita are known only as Prabhu (Master). All three are also known as Gosvami (Master of the Senses). Gadadhara is called by the title Dvija (Brahmana), and Srinivasa is called by the title Pandita (Learned Scholar). These are the titles of the members of the Panca-tattva.

clues.clue: This incarnation of the personality of Godhead is known as the great master and ocean of mercy.
This incarnation of the personality of Godhead is known by the title of master of the senses.
He is an incarnation of the personality of Godhead who carries the title of master of senses.
This member of the group is recognized by the title brahmana in the holy scriptures.
This member of the group holds the title of learned scholar within the holy assembly.
This is the collective name for the divine group comprising these five specific and holy personalities.

13.verse_text: In this regard Srila Svarupa Damodara Gosvami has explained that the title Mahaprabhu should be used only for Lord Caitanya, although the title Prabhu may be used for Lord Caitanya, Lord Nityananda, and Lord Advaita. For this reason the devotees address Lord Caitanya as Mahaprabhu, and Lord Nityananda and Lord Advaita as Prabhu.

clues.clue: This associate provided the authoritative explanation on how to use the titles Mahaprabhu and Prabhu correctly.
Devotees exclusively use the title Mahaprabhu for this personality who is also addressed as Prabhu.
This personality is addressed by devotees as Prabhu along with Lord Advaita and Lord Caitanya.
Devotees address this associate of Lord Caitanya as Prabhu along with Lord Nityananda.

14.verse_text: All the associates of Lord Caitanya, Lord Nityananda, and Lord Advaita are very exalted personalities. The association of Lord Nityananda were all cowherd boys during Lord Krsna's advent.

clues.clue: He is the supreme lord who appeared with many exalted associates in the current age.
This supreme lord is served by many exalted associates who were previously cowherd boys in Vraja.
A supreme lord who works with Lord Caitanya and Lord Nityananda alongside many exalted associates.
These eternal associates of Lord Krsna in Vraja appeared as assistants to Lord Nityananda.
The supreme lord in whose Vraja pastimes the cowherd boys were his eternal associates.

15.verse_text: The associates of Lord Caitanya at Navadvipa are considered the most exalted.

clues.clue: This central figure of Gaura-lila is the person around whom all the associates at Navadvipa gathered.
This is the specific abode where the most exalted associates of Lord Caitanya reside together.
These individuals are recognized as the most exalted group residing within the town of Navadvipa.

16.verse_text: The next most exalted are the Lord's associates at Jagannath Puri, and after them are the great souls who met Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu in His journeys to South India and other places.

clues.clue: This personality traveled extensively to South India and met many great souls during His journeys.
This city serves as the residence for the many exalted associates of the Supreme Lord.

17.verse_text: In his description of Lord Caitanya, Srila Svarupa Damodara Gosvami has explained that among the exalted associates of the Panca-tattva are devotees that were formerly cowherd boys who performed pastimes with Lord Krsna.

clues.clue: Svarupa Damodara Gosvami provided a detailed description of this personality and his exalted associates.
This personality explained that cowherd boys are present among the associates of the group surrounding Caitanya.
Svarupa Damodara Gosvami describes this specific group as containing several cowherd boys who associated with Caitanya.
These associates of Caitanya previously performed pastimes with Krsna in the spiritual realm of Vraja.
Before the time of Caitanya these specific devotees performed various childhood pastimes with this Supreme Personality.

18.verse_text: Some learned devotees say that Navadvipa is identical with the land of Vrndavana. Others say that Navadvipa is actually the spiritual planet Goloka. Some say that Navadvipa is the planet Svetadvipa, and others say that Navadvipa is actually the spiritual sky of Vaikuntha. All glories to the wonderfully splendid land of Navadvipa.

clues.clue: This wonderfully splendid land is considered identical with the spiritual planet of Goloka and the realm of Vaikuntha.
Learned devotees claim that the land of Navadvipa is identical to this eternal residence of Lord Krsna.
Some devotees state that the spiritual planet Navadvipa is identical to this specific spiritual planet.
According to some opinions, the land of Navadvipa is identical to this spiritual planet.
Some say the land of Navadvipa is actually this spiritual sky where the Lord resides.

19.verse_text: Taking the name Visvambhara, the Supreme Personality of Godhead accepted residence in the town of Navadvipa. He enjoyed many transcendental pastimes there with His exalted associates, and those pastimes have drowned the minds of this universe's residents in great transcendental bliss.

clues.clue: This is the name taken by the Supreme Personality of Godhead while residing in the town of Navadvipa.
The Supreme Lord resided in this specific town where He performed His many transcendental pastimes.

20.verse_text: The same Supreme Personality of Godhead who appeared in Satya-yuga as the white-complexioned Lord Sukla, the same Supreme Personality of Godhead who appeared in the Treta-yuga as the red-complexioned Lord Makhamuk, the same Supreme Personality of Godhead who appeared in the Dvapara-yuga as the dark-complexioned Lord Syamasundara, has appeared in the Kali-yuga as the golden moon of Lord Caitanya, and engaged in the chanting of the holy names, the incarnation of Lord Hari.

clues.clue: The golden personality who appeared in the current age to spread the chanting of holy names.
The white complexioned incarnation of the Supreme Personality of Godhead who appeared in the Satya yuga.
The red complexioned incarnation of the Supreme Personality of Godhead who appeared in the Treta yuga.
The dark complexioned incarnation of the Supreme Personality of Godhead who appeared in the Dvapara yuga.

21.verse_text: In the Kali-yuga there are four Vaisnava sampradayas, known as the Sri, Brahma, Rudra, and Sanaka sampradaya. This is described in the following statement of the Padma Purana: 'In the Kali-yuga four Vaisnava sampradayas, the Sri, Brahma, Rudra, and Sanaka smapradayas, will purify the earth.'

clues.clue: This sacred text explains the four Vaisnava schools that exist during the current age of Kali.
One of the four schools mentioned in the text that cleanses the earth in Kali yuga.
Along with three others, this tradition serves to purify the world during the present Kali age.

22.verse_text: I shall now begin this book by describing the disciplic succesion descended from Sripada Madhvacarya. Lord Brahma, the creator of the universe became the disciple of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Narayana. Brahma's disciple was Narada. Narada's disciple was Vyasa. Vyasa then transmitted transcendental knowledge to his disciple Sukadeva. Sukadeva taught the same knowledge to his many disciples and grand-disciples in this world. The famous Madhvacarya received initiation from Vyasa personally. Madhvacaya carefully studied all the Vedas from Vyasa, and later wrote his book Mayavada-sata-dusani, where he proved that the Absolute Truth is the Supreme Person, full of all transcendental qualities, and not the qualityless impersonal Brahman. Madhvacarya's disciple was the exalted Padmanabhacarya. Padmanabhacarya's disciple was Narahari. Narahari's disciple was Madhava Dvija. Madhava Dvija's disciple was Aksobhya. Aksobhya's disciple was Jayatirtha. Jayatirtha's disciple was Jnanasindhu. Jnanasindhu's disciple was Mahanidhi. Mahanidhi's disciple was Vidyanidhi. Vidyanidhi's disciple was Rajendra. Rajendra's disciple was Jayadharma Muni. Among Jayadharma Muni's disciples was Sriman Visnupuri, the famous author of the Bhakti-ratnavali. Another disciple of Jayadharma was Brahmana Purusottama. Purusottama's disciple was Vyasatirtha, who wrote the famous book Sri Visnu-samhita. Vyasatirtha's disciple was Sriman Laksmipati, who was like a great reservoir of the nectar of devotional service. Laksmipati's disciple was Madhavendra Puri, a great preacher of devotional service. Madhavendra Puri was the incarnation of a kalpa-vrksa tree in the abode of Vraja. This tree bears as its fruits the mellows of servitude to Lord Krsna, friendship with Lord Krsna, parental love for Lord Krsna, and conjugal love for Lord Krsna.

clues.clue: This personality is the creator of the universe and was a direct disciple of Lord Narayana.
This is the Supreme Personality of Godhead who accepted the creator of the universe as a disciple.
He is a famous sage who received transcendental knowledge as a disciple of Lord Brahma.
He is the disciple of Narada Muni who transmitted transcendental knowledge to his own student Sukadeva.
He is the disciple of Vyasadeva who taught transcendental knowledge to many students in this world.
He received personal initiation from Vyasadeva and wrote the book proving the Absolute is a Person.
This is the book written by Madhvacarya to prove the Absolute Truth is the Supreme Person.
He is a personality known as an exalted disciple of Madhvacarya.
He is the disciple of Padmanabhacarya in the disciplic succession.
He is the disciple of Narahari and sits between him and Aksobhya in the succession.
He is the disciple of Madhava Dvija who later instructed Jayatirtha.
He is the disciple of Aksobhya and the teacher of Jnanasindhu.
He is the disciple of Jayatirtha and the teacher of Mahanidhi.

He is the disciple of Rajendra and the teacher of Sriman Visnupuri.
He is the disciple of Jayadharma Muni and famous author of the Bhakti-ratnavali.
This is the famous book authored by the disciple of Jayadharma Muni named Sriman Visnupuri.
This brahmana was a disciple of Jayadharma Muni and the teacher of Vyasatirtha.
He is the disciple of Purusottama and the author of the book Sri Visnu-samhita.
This is the famous book written by the disciple of Purusottama named Vyasatirtha.
He is the disciple of Vyasatirtha and a reservoir of the nectar of devotional service.
He is a great preacher of devotional service and a disciple of Sriman Laksmipati.
This tree resides in Vraja and represents the nature of Madhavendra Puri.

23.verse_text: Madhavendra Puri's disciple was Sriman Isvara Puri Svami. Isvara Puri carefully understood the mellows of conjugal love for Lord Krsna, and was able to distribute that fruit to others.

clues.clue: This teacher was the spiritual master and guru of the prominent personality named Isvara Puri Svami.
This disciple of Madhavendra Puri was capable of distributing the fruit of conjugal love for Lord Krsna.
He is the personality who is the supreme recipient of the specific mellows of conjugal love.

24.verse_text: Sri Advaita Acarya displayed the sentiments of servitorship and friendship for the Lord, and Sriman Ranga Puri manifested the sentiment of parental love for Lord Krsna.

clues.clue: This personality displayed the specific sentiments of servitorship and friendship for the Supreme Lord.
This person manifested the specific sentiment of parental love for the Supreme Lord Krsna.
He is the Supreme Lord who accepted the sentiment of parental love from Sriman Ranga Puri.

25.verse_text: Lord Caitanya accepted Sriman Isvara Puri as His spiritual master. The Lord proceeded to flood the entire world with spontaneous transcendental love for Krsna.

clues.clue: He accepted the guidance of the spiritual master and flooded the world with love for the Supreme.
He acted as the spiritual guide who initiated Lord Caitanya into the service of the Supreme.
He is the Supreme personality who was the target of the intense love spread by Lord Caitanya.

26.verse_text: Lord Caitanya accepted Srimati Radharani's golden complexion and ecstatic love for Lord Krsna, both of which had never before been seen in this world. Lord Caitanya was actually Lord Krsna, the son of Maharaja Nanda. He appeared like a great ocean of nectar flooding the entire world.

clues.clue: This personality is the son of Saci who appeared as a golden ocean of nectar.
This personality is the source of the golden complexion and ecstatic love accepted by Lord Caitanya.
This personality is the son of Maharaja Nanda who is also known as the beloved of Radharani.
He is the father of Lord Krsna and a resident of the land of Vraja.

27.verse_text: When Lord Vasudeva, the first member of the Catur-vyuha, saw the dancing of the Gandharvas at Dvaraka Puri, His mind became agitated with the desire to dance as they did. In order to fulfill this desire, Lord Vasudeva entered the transcendental body of Lord Caitanya, to participate in the Lord's pastimes of ecstatic dancing.

clues.clue: This primary member of the Catur-vyuha entered the body of Lord Caitanya to fulfill a desire to dance.
The place where the Gandharvas performed their dancing, which inspired the Catur-vyuha deity to join the pastimes.
The personality whose transcendental body became the site for the ecstatic dancing pastimes of the Catur-vyuha deity.

28.verse_text: The Supreme Personality of Godhead who remains at Dvaraka also entered the body of Lord Caitanya, the son of Saci-devi, and various other incarnations of the Supreme Lord also entered Lord Caitanya's body, all of them present there at the same time.

clues.clue: This Supreme Personality of Godhead remains in the holy city of Dvaraka and entered the body of Caitanya.
This location is described as the eternal abode where the Supreme Personality of Godhead resides.
This son of Saci-devi served as the vessel for many incarnations of the Supreme Lord simultaneously.

29.verse_text: All these incarnations entered the body of Lord Caitanya, just as the black incarnation Lord Syama formerly entered the transcendental body of Lord Krsna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

clues.clue: He is the personality into whom all incarnations, such as the black incarnation, entered.
This black incarnation formerly entered the transcendental body of the Supreme Personality of Godhead.
He is the Supreme Personality of Godhead who received the black incarnation into his body.

30.verse_text: Although the various incarnations of the Lord, who all manifested inconceivable transcendental qualities, were situated in various parts of the spiritual and material worlds, nevertheless, by the power of the Lord's yogamaya energy, They all assembled together within the transcendental form of Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu.

clues.clue: He is the personality who served as the central point for the assembly of all divine incarnations.
He is the original source from whom all transcendental incarnations manifest with their unique divine qualities.
She is the specific energy of the Lord responsible for gathering all incarnations within the form of Caitanya.

31.verse_text: These inconceivable workings of the yogamaya potency cannot be understood by the limited conceptual power of the conditioned soul. This is confirmed by the following statement of Srila Vyasadeva in the Prabhasa-khanda of the Mahabharata: 'The activities of the Personality of Godhead are inconceivable. Ordinary material logic does not have the strength to understand them.'

clues.clue: He is the author of the Mahabharata who explains how material logic cannot understand divine activities.
This specific scripture describes the inconceivable nature of the activities performed by the Supreme Personality of Godhead.
This potency is described as having inconceivable workings that the limited human mind cannot fully comprehend.
The scriptures state that the activities of this entity are completely inconceivable to ordinary human logical thought.

32.verse_text: The various incarnations entered the transcendental body of Lord Caitanya just as formerly Lord Parasurama entered the body of Lord Ramacandra. Narada and other eternal devotees of the Lord also followed Him into this world to perform pastimes with Him in various places, just as the personified Vedas also did.

clues.clue: This is the supreme personality whose transcendental body was entered by various incarnations to perform pastimes.
He is the ancient incarnation who once entered the body of the king known as Rama.
He is the king who served as the vessel for the entry of the incarnation Parasurama.
He is the eternal devotee who followed the Lord into this world to participate in pastimes.

33.verse_text: According to their individual sentiments of ecstatic love for Lord Krsna, the various eternal devotees of the Lord assumed various roles in the Lord's pastimes in Vraja.

clues.clue: This personality is the central recipient of all the intense ecstatic love expressed by his various eternal followers.
These individuals take on specific roles during the pastimes occurring within the spiritual region of Vraja.

34.verse_text: Just as the moon of Lord Caitanya was about to rise in this material world, the Lord said to Advaita Acarya: 'Some devotees are attached to Me in the spirit of servitude, some are attached to Me in the spirit of friendship, and others are attached to Me with a mixture of the spirits of servitude and friendship. Some have firm faith and love for My forms as Sri Sri Radha-Madhava, and others are attached to My form as the king of Dvaraka. Some are attached to My various incarnations and thus display various kinds of love for me. I shall show them how to love Me as the residents of Vraja did.'

clues.clue: This personality planned to demonstrate how to love the Lord just like the residents of Vraja.
He was the trusted confidant of the Lord who received specific instructions regarding the different devotional spirits.
Some devotees possess strong faith and deep love for these specific forms of the Lord.
This is the specific form of the Lord to which some devotees are primarily attached.

35.verse_text: The cowherd man named Parjanya who was Krsna's paternal grandfather in Vrndavana, took birth in the town of Sri Hatta during Lord Caitanya's pastimes. Parjanya took the name Upendra Misra and became the father of seven sons.

clues.clue: In Vrndavana, he served as the paternal grandfather of the Supreme Personality of Godhead known as Krsna.
He appeared in Sri Hatta and acted as the father of seven sons during the pastimes of Caitanya.

36.verse_text: The gopi named Variyasi-devi, who was Lord Krsna's paternal grandmother in Vraja, appeared as Kamalavati-devi during Lord Caitanya's advent.

He is the Supreme Personality of Godhead who manifested his pastimes in the land of Vraja.

37.verse_text: Srimati Yasoda-devi and Vraja's king Nanda, who had been like two great oceans of the nectar of love for Krsna during the Lord's pastimes in Vrndavana, appeared during Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Srimati Saci-devi and Sriman Jagannatha Purandara. Of this there is no doubt.

38-39.verse_text: Some devotees say that the two demigods Aditi and Kasyapa, who incarnated as Kausalya and Dasaratha, Prsni and Sutapa, and also as Devaki and Vasudeva, the parents of Balarama and Krsna, also entered the bodies of Saci-devi and Jagannatha. 41.verse_text: The same Paurnamasi who greatly pleased Lord Govinda in Vraja appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes as Srila Govinda Acarya, the expert author of songs and poetry.

clues.clue: This personality appeared in Lord Caitanya's pastimes after having greatly pleased Lord Govinda in Vraja.
The lord of Vraja who was greatly pleased by the presence of Paurṇamāsī.

42.verse_text: Ambika, the nurse who fed infant Krsna with her own breast milk in Vraja, appeared during Lord Caitany's pastimes as Malini-devi, the wife of Srivasa Pandita.

208.verse_text: Someone may consider that among these devotees some are greatly exalted and others are a little less. I will not make any such distinctions. For me all these great souls are equal.

clues.clue: The author considers all of these individual associates of the Lord to be equally exalted.

209.verse_text: Ciranjiva and Sulocana lived in the village of Srihatta. They were completely surrendered to Lord Caitanya, and because they were the friends and helpers of Narahari dasa they were honored and considered great souls.

clues.clue: This individual from the village of Srihatta was a friend of Narahari dasa and surrendered to Caitanya.
A resident of Srihatta who was a helper to Narahari dasa and devoted to Caitanya.
The village where the individuals named Ciranjiva and Sulocana resided during their lives.
The person who acted as a friend and helper to both Ciranjiva and Sulocana.

210.verse_text: The scriptures state: 'A disciple should not address his spiritual master by name'. For this reason I have not mentioned the previous name of my spiritual master, Sri Srinatha Cakravarti.

clues.clue: He is the spiritual master of the author who wrote the Gaura ganoddesha dipika text.
He is the disciple of his spiritual master and the author of this specific scripture.

211.verse_text: All glories to Sri Krsnadeva of Kumara-hatta, the author of the commentary on Sri Bhagavata-samhita. His fame is always splendidly manifest.

clues.clue: The author of the commentary on the work known as the Bhagavata-samhita who lives in Kumara-hatta.
The specific town where the personality known as Krsnadeva maintains his residence.
The specific scriptural work that features a commentary written by the author known as Krsnadeva.

212.verse_text: If I have made any errors in describing the great souls mentioned in this book, I beg those great personalities to please forgive me. Neither Lord Brahma, Lord Siva, nor Lord Ananta Sesa are able to describe completely the exalted virtues of those great souls.

clues.clue: This personality who is the creator of the universe cannot fully describe the virtues of these great souls.
Like others mentioned, this deity is unable to fully describe the exalted virtues of these great souls.
This personality is also unable to fully describe the exalted virtues of the great souls mentioned here.

213.verse_text: I have written this book in such a way that the Mimamsakas, false logicians, and others who relish dry philosophical debate will not be able to understand it, even with the greatest endeavor. Only the devotees, who are learned in the rasa-sastras, and who continually take shelter of Lord Caitanya's lotus feet, will be able to understand this book properly. I have written this book for them.

clues.clue: He is the author who composed this book to hide deep secrets from dry philosophical thinkers.
This book is intended for those learned in rasa-sastras and remains inaccessible to Mimamsakas and false logicians.
The readers of this book must take shelter of his lotus feet to properly understand the content.

214.verse_text: I pray that this book I have written, the Sri Gaura-ganoddesa-dipika (a lamp to see the associates of Lord Caitanya), may shine in the homes of the blissful devotees of the Lord.

clues.clue: This book written by Kavi Karnapura serves as a lamp to identify the associates of Lord Caitanya.
The associates of this Lord are illuminated by the information provided in the book by Kavi Karnapura.

215.verse_text: This book was written in the year 1498 of the Saka era (corresponding to 1576 A.D.). I pray that those great devotees whose minds are immersed in the nectar of Lord Caitanyacandra's pastimes may read this book critically and correct all its faults.

clues.clue: He is the author who requests great devotees to read this book and correct any of its faults.
He is the Lord whose pastimes are described as immersing the minds of devotees in this written work.
This book was written in the Saka year 1498 and is subject to critical reading by great devotees.
